title: WSL2PodmanAdapter implementation (DEFAULT adapter)
goal: Land the WSL2PodmanAdapter — rootless Podman containers running inside WSL2 — as the operator-default SandboxAdapter implementation per spec §3.5.2. Cross-machine portable, near-native NVIDIA CUDA via the WSL2 CUDA driver, bind-mounts large GGUF files without copy. Default adapter for the disk-agnostic portability requirement [GLOBAL-PORTABILITY-001].
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/wsl2_podman/{mod.rs, adapter.rs, podman_cli.rs, wsl_detection.rs, gpu_probe.rs}; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/wsl2_podman_adapter_tests.rs (gated by `#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "wsl2-integration"), ignore)]` so the suite runs in CI only when the WSL2 host is wired). Cargo.toml additions: tokio (process + io), which, tempfile.
unblock_conditions: Shared types + SandboxAdapter trait merged; selection registry not required for this MT (adapter is implemented standalone, then registered in MT-048 wiring).
implementation_notes: Per RW-1 synthesis (refinement.json research_basis_plan[0]) WSL2+Podman scored 32/40 on the Windows landscape scan and is the operator-locked DEFAULT. Implementation strategy: shell out to the `wsl.exe` CLI to enter the configured WSL2 distro (default: `Ubuntu`, configurable via `settings.local_models.wsl2_distro`), then run `podman --remote=false --rootless` commands inside the distro. NO direct libpod bindings (avoids C ABI risk per RW-1 maintenance scoring). adapter.rs implements `SandboxAdapter`: (1) `spawn`: builds a `podman run --rm -d --userns=keep-id --net=$NET --read-only --tmpfs /tmp` argv from ProcessSpec, applies bind specs as `-v host:guest:mode`, applies env via `-e`, applies resource limits via `--memory --cpus --pids-limit`, applies net policy (DenyAll -> `--network=none`; LoopbackOnly -> `--network=slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns,allow_host_loopback=true` plus per-process iptables localhost-only seed; Allowlist -> create an ephemeral CNI network per process plus iptables allowlist seed), captures the container id as `sandbox_internal_id`, mints ProcessHandle with UUID v7. (2) `exec`: `podman exec <container_id> <argv>` with stdin/stdout/stderr piped via tokio::process; returns ExecResult with duration_ms measured by Instant. (3) `fs_bind`: post-spawn binds are NOT supported by Podman (bind specs are spawn-time); MUST return SandboxAdapterError::SpawnFailed{ reason: "post-spawn fs_bind unsupported on Podman; declare in ProcessSpec.binds" }. (4) `net_policy`: post-spawn policy reapply uses `podman network connect/disconnect` (Allowlist updates) and iptables rules; DenyAll forces network removal. (5) `kill`: maps Signal::Term -> `podman stop --time 10`, Signal::Kill -> `podman kill --signal KILL`, Signal::Int -> `podman kill --signal INT`. (6) `status`: `podman inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}'` parsed into ProcessStatus. (7) `exit_code`: `podman inspect --format '{{.State.ExitCode}}'`. (8) `capabilities()`: returns AdapterCapabilities { adapter_id: "wsl2_podman", filesystem_isolation_strength: Strong, network_isolation_strength: Strong, gpu_passthrough: NvidiaCuda (detected via gpu_probe.rs running `nvidia-smi` inside the distro; falls back to None if probe fails; cached across calls per HBR-SWARM-001 perf), stdio_throughput_class: High, win32_native_fidelity: false, cross_machine_portable: true }. wsl_detection.rs verifies WSL2 is installed (`wsl.exe --status` parsed for `Default Version: 2`) and the configured distro is registered (`wsl.exe -l -v`); SpawnFailed loud on detection failure. gpu_probe.rs runs `wsl.exe -d <distro> -- nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name --format=csv,noheader` and parses; cached for 60s; HBR-SWARM-001 concurrency-safe via OnceCell + Mutex. Integration tests (gated): spawn a `docker.io/library/alpine:3.20` container, run `echo hello`, assert exit code 0 + stdout=="hello\n"; NetPolicy::DenyAll prevents outbound DNS; bind a 1KB temp file ReadOnly and verify writes inside guest fail with EROFS.
risk_if_missed: Without the default adapter Handshake's local-model stack cannot box any process; KERNEL-004 cannot ship.
validator_focus: (a) Adapter implements SandboxAdapter without leaking podman or wsl types into the trait surface; (b) net_policy DenyAll actually blocks outbound DNS in integration test; (c) capabilities().cross_machine_portable==true; (d) bind-mount ReadOnly mode is enforced (guest write returns EROFS); (e) UUID v7 in ProcessHandle.id; (f) gpu_probe is cached and concurrent-safe under HBR-SWARM-001.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: WSL2 + distro detection at adapter construction fails loud if WSL2 is absent; no silent local-only fallback.
Net-policy DenyAll integration test asserts an outbound `getent hosts` inside the guest fails; prevents the most-common 'sandbox advertised but doesn't enforce' regression.
Bind ReadOnly integration test asserts `echo x > /mnt/readonly/foo` returns EROFS; required by spec §3.6.3 (gguf_root mounted read-only).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: WSL2PodmanAdapter spawns/exec/kill/status work and capabilities are honest about win32 fidelity, but the NetPolicy::LoopbackOnly path silently maps to --network=none (loopback is unreachable in netnone, so the policy name lies about what is enforced) and the test suite endorses that downgrade.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: WSL2PodmanAdapter spawns/exec/kill/status work and capabilities are honest about win32 fidelity, but the NetPolicy::LoopbackOnly path silently maps to --network=none (loopback is unreachable in netnone, so the policy name lies about what is enforced) and the test suite endorses that downgrade.
